free_text: Experimental and theoretical excitation functions for
Br-nat (P,X) reactions.
Last checking has been done.
Regarding to Br-77 production.
The separated-sector cyclotron at the
National Accelerator Centre.
ALICE-code.
100.9-, 66.8-, 40.4 MeV beams were used.       O
The various foil stacks were irradiated for 1
hour.
Thin samples, 100-200 mg/cm**2 thickness and 20-mm in
diameter, were made from KBr salt powder, presssed
into tables between two thin, 4.61-mg/cm**2 thick, Al
foils to prevent the escape of gaseous isotopes.
For the measurement of proton flux and for additional
energy degradation, each target foil in the stack was
followed by a high-purity Al monitor foil and one or
more high-purity Cu foils. In proton energy range below
25-MeV the Al monitor foils were replaced by Cu
monitor foils.
The thicknesses of the stacks were sufficient to
provide satisfactory energy overlaps of the three
different data sets.
The total uncertainties in the cross-section
values calculated from the experimental data were
estimated by summing the contributing uncertainties in
quadrature.  Each uncertainty value consists of a part
that varies from foil to foil, due to counting
statistics and photo peak integration as well as a
constant part of 8.1% as previously described by Steyn
et al.  See REL-REF.
Data for Kr-77
are consistenly lower than the measurements obtained in
this work.
There is a
very
good agreement between the cross section for Kr-79
published by COLLE and data obtained in this work.
